issues.main_description_html:
[Tour Detail] Integrate Storage Full (Flutter Migration)

Migration context: Port of GoThru 360 Virtual Tour Maker (React Native / Expo) → Flutter.
Source reference: docs/analyze.md §2.7 (per-user storage quota), §3.4 (GET /v1/users/detail-storage). The RN app also checks device free disk space before capture/import. "Storage Full" must block actions that would exceed the user's GoThru quota and/or the device storage, with a clear message.

Goal

Implement the "Storage Full" state in Tour Detail: when the user's GoThru storage quota (or device storage) is exhausted, block storage-consuming actions (capture/import/upload) and show a clear, actionable message.

Scope

  • Detect full storage: GoThru quota used ≥ total (via detail-storage) and/or device free space below the required threshold.
  • Block storage-consuming actions (add panorama from phone/camera, capture, upload) when full.
  • Show a clear "Storage Full" message with guidance (e.g. free up space / upgrade / buy more).
  • Re-check after the user frees space (state can recover).

Out of Scope

  • "Storage Almost Full" warning (separate task); the purchase/upgrade flow itself.

Acceptance Criteria

  • Full storage (quota exhausted and/or device full) is detected reliably.
  • Storage-consuming actions are blocked while full, with a clear message + guidance.
  • The message is dismissible/actionable; state recovers once space is freed.
  • Requires connectivity for quota check; device-space check works offline.
  • Labels via i18n.

🧪 Test Scenarios (for QA)

TC-1 — Quota full blocks actions (happy path)

  1. Use an account whose GoThru storage is at/over quota.
  2. Try to add/capture/upload a panorama.
  3. Expected: Action blocked; clear "Storage Full" message with guidance.

TC-2 — Device storage full

  1. Fill the device storage below the required threshold.
  2. Try to capture/import.
  3. Expected: Blocked with a clear device-storage-full message.

TC-3 — Recovery after freeing space

  1. From a full state, free up quota/device space, re-check.
  2. Expected: Actions are re-enabled; message clears.

TC-4 — Accurate detection

  1. Compare the app's full/not-full decision against actual quota/device values.
  2. Expected: No false "full" when space exists; no allowing actions when truly full.

TC-5 — Offline behavior

  1. Trigger while offline.
  2. Expected: Device-space check still applies; quota check handled gracefully (no crash).

TC-6 — Localization

  1. Switch language.
  2. Expected: Message/guidance translated.

Platforms: Android + iOS
Regression watch: non-storage actions remain usable while storage is full.

issues.display_title: Issue #94: [Tour Detail] Integrate Storage Almost Full
issues.main_description_html:
[Tour Detail] Integrate Storage Almost Full (Flutter Migration)

Migration context: Port of GoThru 360 Virtual Tour Maker (React Native / Expo) → Flutter.
Source reference: docs/analyze.md §2.7 (per-user storage quota), §3.4 (GET /v1/users/detail-storage). Before reaching the hard "Storage Full" block, the user should get a warning as their GoThru quota (or device storage) approaches its limit.

Goal

Implement the "Storage Almost Full" warning in Tour Detail: when the user's GoThru quota (or device storage) crosses a near-full threshold, show a non-blocking warning so they can act before hitting the full state.

Scope

  • Detect near-full: usage ≥ a configurable threshold (e.g. ~80–90%) of GoThru quota and/or device storage.
  • Show a non-blocking warning (banner/indicator) with usage and guidance (free up / upgrade / buy more).
  • Do NOT block actions (that's the "Storage Full" task); warning is informational.
  • Avoid nagging: show appropriately (e.g. once per session / until dismissed), and clear when usage drops below the threshold.

Out of Scope

  • The hard "Storage Full" block (separate task); the purchase/upgrade flow.

Acceptance Criteria

  • Near-full state (≥ threshold of quota and/or device) is detected.
  • A non-blocking warning shows usage + guidance; actions still work.
  • Warning frequency is sensible (not shown on every action); dismissible.
  • Warning clears when usage falls below the threshold.
  • Labels via i18n.

🧪 Test Scenarios (for QA)

TC-1 — Warning at threshold (happy path)

  1. Use an account whose storage is near (but under) the limit (e.g. ~85%).
  2. Open Tour Detail.
  3. Expected: A non-blocking "Storage Almost Full" warning appears with usage + guidance.

TC-2 — Actions still allowed

  1. With the warning shown, add/capture/upload a panorama.
  2. Expected: Action proceeds (warning does not block).

TC-3 — Below threshold = no warning

  1. Use an account well under the threshold.
  2. Expected: No warning shown.

TC-4 — Transition to full

  1. Cross from almost-full into full (paired task).
  2. Expected: Warning gives way to the blocking "Storage Full" state.

TC-5 — Clears after freeing space

  1. From almost-full, free space below the threshold.
  2. Expected: Warning disappears.

TC-6 — Not naggy / dismissible

  1. Dismiss the warning; continue using the app.
  2. Expected: It does not reappear on every action (per the agreed frequency rule).

TC-7 — Localization

  1. Switch language.
  2. Expected: Warning/guidance translated.

Platforms: Android + iOS
Regression watch: the warning never blocks actions (only "Storage Full" does).

[App] Setup iOS (Flutter Migration)

Migration context: Port of GoThru 360 Virtual Tour Maker (React Native / Expo) → Flutter.
Source reference: RN/Expo iOS config (app.json iOS section, GoogleService-Info.plist, Apple sign-in, permissions) — see docs/analyze.md §1 (iOS bundle com.gothru.streeteditor), §2.8 (Apple sign-in), §3.5 (camera/WiFi/local-network). This task sets up the iOS platform target of the Flutter app so it builds, runs, and has the required capabilities/permissions.

Goal

Set up the iOS side of the Flutter app so it builds and runs on a physical iOS device, with the correct bundle identifier, signing, capabilities, and the permission usage descriptions the app needs.

Scope

  • Configure the iOS Runner: bundle id (com.gothru.streeteditor), display name, version/build, deployment target.
  • Signing & capabilities: team/provisioning, Apple Sign-In, networking (local network for camera), background modes if needed.
  • Info.plist usage descriptions: Camera, Photo Library (read + add), Location (when-in-use/always for WiFi info), Local Network (camera connectivity), Motion (sensors).
  • App Transport Security exceptions required for the camera/local endpoints (parity with RN NSAppTransportSecurity).
  • Google config (GoogleService-Info.plist) and Google/Apple sign-in URL schemes if used.
  • Verify a successful build + launch on a physical device (and on a simulator where applicable).

Out of Scope

  • Feature implementation (capture, upload, etc.); Insta360 iOS SDK install (separate task if needed); App Store submission.

Acceptance Criteria

  • iOS app builds successfully (Xcode / flutter build ios) with the correct bundle id and signing.
  • App launches on a physical iOS device without crashing on startup.
  • Required Info.plist usage descriptions are present (camera, photos, location, local network, motion).
  • ATS exceptions configured so camera/local + API endpoints work.
  • Apple Sign-In capability enabled; Google config in place (if used).
  • Permission prompts appear with correct, human-readable descriptions.

🧪 Test Scenarios (for QA)

TC-1 — iOS build succeeds

  1. Build the iOS app (Xcode / flutter build ios).
  2. Expected: Build completes with correct bundle id/signing; no missing-capability errors.

TC-2 — Launch on device

  1. Install on a physical iPhone and cold-start.
  2. Expected: App launches to the normal flow; no startup crash.

TC-3 — Permission prompts

  1. Trigger camera, photo, location, and local-network usage.
  2. Expected: Each prompt appears with a clear, correct description string.

TC-4 — Local network / ATS

  1. Exercise a camera/local-endpoint or HTTP API call.
  2. Expected: Connections work (ATS/local-network not blocking); no transport errors.

TC-5 — Apple Sign-In capability

  1. Verify the Apple Sign-In capability is present/usable (where wired).
  2. Expected: Capability available; no entitlement errors.

TC-6 — Clean install

  1. Delete and reinstall the app.
  2. Expected: Installs and runs cleanly; permissions re-prompt as expected.

Platforms: iOS (physical device required for camera/local-network/sensors).
Regression watch: Android build/run remains unaffected by iOS setup.

[App] Setup App Icon (Flutter Migration)

Migration context: Port of GoThru 360 Virtual Tour Maker (React Native / Expo) → Flutter.
Source reference: RN/Expo app icon config (app.json: icon, Android adaptiveIcon with foregroundImage + backgroundColor, iOS icon) — see docs/analyze.md §1. This task sets up the app launcher icon for the Flutter app on both platforms, matching the existing GoThru branding.

Goal

Set up the app launcher icon for the Flutter app on Android and iOS, using the GoThru brand icon, so the installed app shows the correct icon across devices/launchers.

Scope

  • Source the GoThru brand icon asset(s) at the required base resolution.
  • Generate/configure all required icon sizes (e.g. via flutter_launcher_icons or manual asset catalogs).
  • Android: adaptive icon (foreground + background) + legacy/round icons across mipmap densities.
  • iOS: AppIcon asset catalog across all required sizes (no alpha channel).
  • Verify the icon renders correctly on home screen, app switcher, and settings.

Sub-tasks

  • App icon — Android (adaptive + legacy/round, all densities)
  • App icon — iOS (AppIcon set, all sizes, no alpha)

Out of Scope

  • Splash screen (separate concern); notification icons; rebranding/new icon design.

Acceptance Criteria

  • Android launcher icon set (adaptive foreground/background + legacy/round) across all densities.
  • iOS AppIcon set complete for all required sizes (no transparency).
  • Installed app shows the correct GoThru icon on both platforms.
  • Icon renders crisply (no blur/pixelation/clipping) on home screen, app switcher, settings.
  • No default Flutter icon remaining anywhere.

🧪 Test Scenarios (for QA)

TC-1 — Android icon (happy path)

  1. Build & install on an Android device.
  2. Expected: Correct GoThru icon on the home screen and app drawer; adaptive icon shapes correctly per launcher.

TC-2 — Android adaptive masking

  1. View the icon under different launcher mask shapes (circle/squircle/rounded).
  2. Expected: Foreground stays centered/uncropped; background fills correctly.

TC-3 — iOS icon (happy path)

  1. Build & install on an iPhone.
  2. Expected: Correct GoThru icon on home screen, Spotlight, and Settings.

TC-4 — iOS no-alpha / sizing

  1. Inspect the iOS icon.
  2. Expected: No black/transparent edges; all required sizes present (no missing-icon warnings).

TC-5 — No default icon

  1. Check home screen, app switcher, settings on both platforms.
  2. Expected: No leftover default Flutter logo anywhere.

TC-6 — Crispness across densities

  1. View on low- and high-density devices.
  2. Expected: Icon is crisp; no blur/pixelation/clipping.

Platforms: Android + iOS (physical devices recommended).
Regression watch: app build/run unaffected; splash screen (if any) unchanged.

[Login] Integrate Login Apple (Flutter Migration)

Migration context: Port of GoThru 360 Virtual Tour Maker (React Native / Expo) → Flutter.
Source reference: RN uses Apple Authentication (expo-apple-authentication; iOS entitlement com.apple.developer.applesignin) alongside Google/email auth and the GoThru session (token/tokenApil/gothru_uid); see docs/analyze.md §2.8, §3.4. Completes the auth set with the existing [Login] Email and [Login] Google tasks.

Goal

Implement Sign in with Apple in the Flutter app: the user authenticates with Apple, the app exchanges/validates with the GoThru API, and the session is persisted.

Scope

  • "Sign in with Apple" button (shown on iOS; per Apple guidelines) → native Apple auth flow.
  • Obtain Apple credential (identity token / authorization code), authenticate with GoThru, store token/tokenApil/gothru_uid.
  • Handle first-login name/email (Apple only returns these once) and the private-relay email case.
  • Persist session across restarts; route to Main on success; handle cancel/failure.

Out of Scope

  • Email and Google sign-in (separate tasks); account deletion.

Acceptance Criteria

  • Apple sign-in completes and creates a valid GoThru session.
  • Session/tokens stored securely; session persists across restarts.
  • First-login name/email captured; private-relay email handled.
  • User-cancelled sign-in returns cleanly (no error spam).
  • Failures (network/denied) surfaced; on success lands on Main.
  • iOS Apple Sign-In capability/entitlement configured.

🧪 Test Scenarios (for QA)

TC-1 — Apple sign-in (happy path)

  1. On iOS, tap Sign in with Apple → authenticate.
  2. Expected: Signs in; lands on Main; session stored.

TC-2 — User cancels

  1. Start Apple sign-in and cancel.
  2. Expected: Returns to login cleanly; no error/crash.

TC-3 — First login captures identity

  1. First-time Apple sign-in.
  2. Expected: Name/email captured on this first response (handles Apple's one-time return).

TC-4 — Private relay email

  1. Use "Hide My Email".
  2. Expected: Relay email handled; account works.

TC-5 — Session persistence

  1. Sign in with Apple, relaunch the app.
  2. Expected: Still signed in.

TC-6 — Network error

  1. Attempt sign-in offline / simulate failure.
  2. Expected: Clear error; retry possible.

TC-7 — Capability present

  1. Verify the iOS Apple Sign-In entitlement is configured.
  2. Expected: No entitlement errors; button works.

Platforms: iOS (primary; physical device recommended). Show/hide per platform guidelines.
Regression watch: email and Google login still work alongside Apple.

issues.display_title: Issue #99: [Camera360] Install SDK Insta360 for iOS
issues.main_description_html:
[Camera360] Install SDK Insta360 for iOS (Flutter Migration)

Migration context: Port of GoThru 360 Virtual Tour Maker (React Native / Expo) → Flutter.
Source reference: RN talks to Insta360 cameras over OSC/WiFi (src/services/insta360.service.js, fixed AP http://192.168.42.1:80); see docs/analyze.md §2.1, §3.5. Integrating the official Insta360 iOS SDK enables richer/more reliable camera control beyond raw OSC. iOS counterpart to the Android SDK install task.

Goal

Install and wire up the Insta360 iOS SDK into the Flutter app's iOS target so the native module is available to the Dart/JS layer, builds cleanly, and runs on a physical iOS device.

Scope

  • Add the Insta360 iOS SDK dependency (CocoaPods / framework) to the iOS Runner.
  • Configure required iOS bits: Info.plist (local network + Bonjour services, camera/photo as needed), useFrameworks / static linking parity, min deployment target.
  • Expose a minimal Flutter ↔ iOS platform-channel entry point so the SDK can be initialized from Dart.
  • Verify a successful iOS build + launch on a physical device with the SDK linked.

Out of Scope

  • Full capture flow on top of the SDK (separate task); Android SDK install (separate task); App Store submission.

Acceptance Criteria

  • Insta360 iOS SDK added and resolves during the iOS build (pod install / framework linked).
  • iOS build succeeds with the SDK included; app launches on a physical device without crashing.
  • Required Info.plist entries present (local network + Bonjour services for camera connectivity).
  • SDK can be initialized from Dart via a platform channel (logged success).
  • No regression to the existing OSC-over-WiFi path or to the Android build.

🧪 Test Scenarios (for QA)

TC-1 — iOS build with SDK

  1. Run the iOS build (pod install + Xcode / flutter build ios).
  2. Expected: Builds without dependency/linking errors related to the Insta360 SDK.

TC-2 — Launch on device

  1. Install on a physical iPhone and cold-start.
  2. Expected: App opens to the normal flow; no crash/ANR attributable to the SDK.

TC-3 — SDK initialization

  1. Trigger the SDK init via the platform channel (camera/connection screen).
  2. Expected: SDK initializes; success logged; no native crash.

TC-4 — Local network permission

  1. Trigger camera connectivity that uses the local network.
  2. Expected: iOS local-network prompt appears; with Bonjour services configured, discovery/connection works.

TC-5 — Existing OSC flow regression

  1. Connect to an Insta360 over WiFi as before (OSC).
  2. Expected: Existing connect/capture still works.

TC-6 — Clean reinstall

  1. Delete and reinstall the app.
  2. Expected: Installs and runs cleanly; no leftover linkage issues.

Platforms: iOS only (physical device + a physical Insta360 camera required for TC-4/TC-5).
Regression watch: Android build/run unaffected by the iOS SDK install.
